remember: Students will be able to recall the major Bhakti and Sufi saints and their contributions.
Students will be able to identify the key features of Bhakti and Sufi movements in India.
Students will be able to list the major literary works of Bhakti and Sufi poets.
understand: Students will be able to explain the social and cultural context in which Bhakti and Sufi movements emerged.
Students will be able to describe the role of Bhakti and Sufi saints in challenging social norms and promoting spiritual values.
Students will be able to compare the similarities and differences between Bhakti and Sufi traditions.
apply: Students will be able to analyze the impact of Bhakti and Sufi movements on Indian society and culture.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of Bhakti and Sufi saints in promoting social change and reform.
Students will be able to apply the principles of Bhakti and Sufi traditions to contemporary social issues.
analyze: Students will be able to critically evaluate the role of power and patronage in shaping Bhakti and Sufi movements.
Students will be able to analyze the tensions and contradictions within Bhakti and Sufi traditions.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of colonialism and modernity on Bhakti and Sufi traditions.

ncert_objectives: To understand the emergence and development of Bhakti and Sufi traditions in India.
To analyze the role of Bhakti and Sufi saints in shaping Indian culture and society.
To evaluate the impact of Bhakti and Sufi movements on Indian history and culture.
board_exam_objectives: To explain the key features of Bhakti and Sufi movements in India.
To describe the role of Bhakti and Sufi saints in promoting spiritual values and challenging social norms.
To analyze the impact of Bhakti and Sufi movements on Indian society and culture.
